svg.font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif
svg.text: Mixed-mode tools: an inline @tool that talks to a domain API + an MCP tool from mcp-web. The system prompt enforces "catalog before recommendation".

g.text: Domain endpoint hit by inline tool
Agent factory — inline + MCP tools mixed in one list
# Pattern: inline = app-specific domain · MCP = shared, reusable across apps
→ IBM Global Catalog · service names, descriptions, URLs
Why inline? IBM Catalog API doesn't fit any existing
MCP server theme; only this app uses it.

svg.text: REQUEST FLOW — POST /ask "Design a HIPAA-compliant queue-based image processor on IBM Cloud"

g.text: Inline tool runs in main.py's process —
no MCP overhead. MCP tool round-trips
over HTTP. To the LLM, both look
Why this matters — the mixed-tool pattern, made concrete
IBM Catalog only this app uses → kept
Catalog is the oracle for what exists.
cost note. Format dictated, not LLM whim.
UI renders cleanly because it's predictable.
